title: Bulk Edit Line of Business and Topics for Multiple Initiatives
story: As a DataTools Pro user managing a large set of initiatives, I want to select multiple initiatives using Shift+click and bulk-edit their Line of Business and Topics through a popup, so that I can efficiently categorize many initiatives at once without editing each one individually.
description:
Adds multi-select and bulk-edit capability to the Initiatives table. Users hold the Shift key to select multiple initiative rows. Once selected, a button appears in the table header showing the count of selected initiatives. Clicking this button opens a popup where the user can assign or change the Line of Business and Topics for all selected initiatives in a single action.

This mirrors the bulk-edit pattern used in other DataTools Pro modules such as the Metrics Glossary.
acceptance_criteria: User can select multiple initiative rows by holding Shift and clicking
Table header displays a button showing the count of currently selected initiatives
Clicking the selection count button opens a popup with Line of Business and Topics fields
User can assign or change Line of Business for all selected initiatives in one action
User can assign or change Topics for all selected initiatives in one action
Bulk edit applies changes to all selected initiatives simultaneously
Selection state clears after a successful bulk edit
ux_changes: Adds Shift+click multi-select to the Initiatives table, a header button showing the selected count, and a bulk-edit popup with Line of Business and Topics dropdowns.
middleware_changes: New batch-update logic accepts an array of initiative IDs with target Line of Business and Topic values, validates the categories, and applies the assignment.
api_changes: Batch update endpoint for initiatives that accepts multiple initiative IDs with Line of Business and Topic assignments and returns updated records.
implementation_notes: Pattern cloned from the Metrics Glossary bulk-edit feature, adapted for the Initiative model and its taxonomy relationships.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the implementation was complete and ready for review.
